title: Converted: Falsifiability and the problem of induction
description: Structured text conversion of legacy contentData[118]. The original legacy slide remains available for comparison.

slides.title: Falsifiability and the problem of induction
body: The process of induction, or generalizing from specific observations to broader principles, is inherently uncertain. Even a theory that has been supported by a large body of evidence could potentially be falsified by new observations, which means that all theories are, in principle, provisional and subject to revision.
